papers.title: Quantifying the Accuracy and Cost Impact of Design Decisions in Budget-Constrained Agentic LLM Search
papers.innovation: Presents a controlled measurement study to quantify the impact of agentic RAG design decisions (search depth, retrieval strategy, completion token limits) on accuracy and cost under explicit budget constraints. This provides empirical evidence for crucial trade-offs in real-world deployments.
papers.benchmarks: Focuses on quantifying the accuracy and cost impact of design decisions in agentic RAG systems, providing a framework for benchmarking specific design choices under budget constraints.
papers.use_case: Optimizing agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for deployment in production environments where computational budgets (e.g., API calls, token usage) are strict. Essential for engineers to make data-driven decisions on system configuration for efficiency and performance.

papers.title: MASEval: Extending Multi-Agent Evaluation from Models to Systems
papers.innovation: Proposes a fundamental shift in evaluation methodologies for multi-agent LLM systems, moving from model-centric benchmarks to a comprehensive system-centric approach. This framework considers the performance of the entire agentic setup, including frameworks and other system components, not just the base LLM.
papers.benchmarks: Criticizes existing 'model-centric' benchmarks and advocates for the development of new, holistic benchmarks that encompass all system components within multi-agent LLM setups.
papers.use_case: Provides a conceptual framework and strong argument for AI engineers to conduct more thorough and realistic evaluations of multi-agent LLM systems. This is critical for building robust, reliable, and performant systems by understanding the true impact of architectural and component choices.

papers.title: MEMO: Memory-Augmented Model Context Optimization for Robust Multi-Turn Multi-Agent LLM Games
papers.innovation: Introduces MEMO, a memory-augmented model context optimization technique that significantly reduces run-to-run variance in multi-turn, multi-agent LLM game evaluations. This improves the reliability of win rate estimates and rankings, addressing a key challenge in evaluating complex agent interactions.
papers.benchmarks: Directly addresses and aims to improve the robustness and reliability of win rate estimates and rankings across repeated tournaments in multi-turn, multi-agent LLM game evaluations.
papers.use_case: Essential for AI engineers who are developing, iterating on, and comparing multi-agent LLM systems, particularly in long-horizon interaction or competitive scenarios. Enables more trustworthy performance metrics, leading to more reliable experimentation and faster development cycles.
